name: Port Scan Detected (SIEM Correlation Needed)
description: Detects a source host scanning a high number of distinct destination ports on a target host within a short timeframe, indicating a port scan, a common reconnaissance technique.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of one of the network connection events (e.g., the most recent one).

parameters.name: SourceIp
parameters.description: The source IP address of the scanner.

parameters.name: DestinationIp
parameters.description: The destination IP address being scanned.

parameters.name: DestinationPort
parameters.description: One of the ports detected as part of the scan.

parameters.name: Protocol
parameters.description: The protocol used for the scan (e.g., TCP, UDP).

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: The action taken by the firewall/IDS (e.g., allowed, blocked).

parameters.name: RuleName
parameters.description: The name of the Sigma rule that triggered the event, if applicable.

parameters.name: SIEM_DISTINCT_COUNT_PORTS
parameters.description: The number of distinct destination ports identified by the SIEM aggregation (e.g., 65).

parameters.name: SIEM_TIMEFRAME
parameters.description: The time window over which the distinct ports were counted (e.g., '5 minutes').

parameters.name: SIEM_FIRST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the first event in the correlated group.

parameters.name: SIEM_LAST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last event in the correlated group.
